title: Earnings call transcript: Riot Platforms Q4 2024 sees strong revenue growth
text: Riot reported total revenue of $376.7 million in 2024, representing a 34% increase year over year, driven primarily by higher Bitcoin prices. The company achieved net income of $109.4 million, reversing a prior-year loss of $49.5 million. Gross profit climbed to $147.6 million (51% increase) while non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA reached $463.2 million (116% increase). Bitcoin mining revenue surged 70% to $321 million. Riot ended 2024 holding 17,722 Bitcoin, up 141% from 7,362 in 2023, with CEO Jason Les stating the company purchased bitcoin for the first time in December 2024, adding over 10,000 Bitcoin to the balance sheet. The company increased self-mining hash rate from 12.4 to 31.5 exahash (154% growth), outpacing network growth of 67%. Block Mining acquisition brought 60 megawatts of operations with expansion to 305+ megawatts, while E4A Solutions acquisition provided $52 million for electrical engineering services, generating $28.2 million revenue in 2024. For 2025, Riot expects approximately 22% increase in Bitcoin mining hash rate, targeting 38.4 exahash by year-end, with capital expenditures projected at $198 million. Management is aggressively pursuing AI high-performance computing opportunities at Corsicana and Rockdale facilities, engaging financial advisors Evercore and Northland to evaluate partnerships with hyperscalers seeking large-scale power access.
